questions.question: What is the topic for Week 6?
questions.explanation: The topic provided for Lesson Six is KNOW YOURSELF.

questions.question: Which of the following was the topic for Week 5?
questions.explanation: My Enemy: 'The Flesh' was the topic for Week 5.

questions.question: But you are a chosen generation, a royal ________, a holy nation, His own special people...
questions.explanation: The memory verse (1 Peter 2:9) says 'a royal priesthood'.

questions.question: ...that you may proclaim the praises of Him who called you out of ________ into His marvellous light.
questions.explanation: The verse says 'who called you out of darkness into His marvellous light.'

questions.question: Provide the book, chapter, and verse for this week's memory verse.
questions.explanation: The memory verse is 1 Peter 2:9.

questions.question: Type out the memory verse for Week 6 in full (NKJV).
questions.answer: But you are a chosen generation, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, His own special people, that you may proclaim the praises of Him who called you out of darkness into His marvellous light.
questions.explanation: Full text recall of 1 Peter 2:9.

questions.question: According to the memory verse (1 Peter 2:9), what is the purpose of being a chosen generation and royal priesthood?
options: A. To rule over the nations
C. To proclaim the praises of Him who called you
D. To become wealthy and prosperous
questions.explanation: The verse states 'that you may proclaim the praises of Him who called you out of darkness'.

questions.question: According to the introduction, what is the result of some people being ignorant of who they are?
options: A. They lived happy but simple lives
B. They missed opportunities and failed woefully
D. They sought help from false gods
questions.explanation: The intro states: 'Some people have missed opportunities and failed woefully because they were ignorant of who they are.'

questions.question: According to the introduction, to know yourself is to have adequate knowledge of your personality and the ________ you wield as God’s child.
questions.explanation: The text says 'and the power you wield as God’s child'.

questions.question: Which Bible reference is given in the introduction to support the idea that we are royalty created to shine as a light out of darkness?
questions.explanation: The text cites '(Revelation 5:10)' right after 'knowing full well that you are royalty created to shine as a light out of darkness'.

questions.question: What is the exact heading for Outline I?
questions.explanation: Outline I is titled 'THE MANUFACTURER’S MANUAL'.

questions.question: According to Outline I, who is our manufacturer and what is His manual?
options: A. Our parents; the traditions of men
B. The Holy Spirit; our conscience
D. Society; the law of the land
questions.explanation: The text says 'God is our creator and His manual is His word (the Bible.)'

questions.question: Which scriptures are given to support the idea that we must grow our word base by constantly feeding on God’s word?
questions.explanation: The lesson links feeding on God's word to '(Psalm 1:1-2; 2 Timothy 2:15)'.

questions.question: Which scriptures are given to support obeying God's word beyond face value?
questions.explanation: The text associates obeying beyond face value with '(Psalm 119:11; James 1:23-25)'.

questions.question: The more of the Bible we read, the more life we get and the healthier, stronger, and more joyful we become (Jeremiah 15:16; Proverbs 4:20-22).
questions.explanation: The text explicitly makes this claim based on those verses.

questions.question: According to Outline I, whom did God ordain as a prophet before birth because He knew him before he was formed?
questions.explanation: The lesson notes: 'God told Jeremiah that He knew him before he was formed... ordained him a prophet (Jeremiah 1:5)'.

questions.question: What demands that we know God intimately to discern the reason we were created and see how to fulfil our reason for existence (John 15:5)?
questions.explanation: The text states: 'Wisdom thus demands that we know God intimately...'

questions.question: What is the exact heading for Outline II?
questions.explanation: Outline II is titled 'THE PLACE OF KNOWLEDGE'.

questions.question: How does the Week 6 lesson define 'knowledge' in Outline II?
questions.answer: Knowledge is information. It is facts gathered about something or someone
questions.explanation: The text defines it as 'Knowledge is information. It is facts gathered about something or someone'.

questions.question: Which verses are cited to support the concept that knowledge is facts gathered about something or someone?
questions.explanation: The lesson lists '(1 Samuel 30:11-17; Proverbs 13:16)' for this point.

questions.question: To know yourself is to have the understanding of your make-up, goals, aspirations, and authority in Christ.
questions.explanation: This is a direct statement from Outline II.

questions.question: According to the Week 6 lesson citing 2 Timothy 4:6-7, why is it easy to get to where you are going?
options: A. Because God fights for you
B. When you are familiar with the way
C. If you have the right friends
questions.explanation: The text says 'It is easy to get to where you are going when you are familiar with the way (2 Timothy. 4:6-7).'

questions.question: Which Bible character's intentionality and determination for success is highlighted in Esther 4:16?
questions.explanation: Esther 4:16 is cited to show intentionality about success.

questions.question: According to Outline II, what tool can be of help to understand your emotions, desires, and ability?
questions.explanation: The text states: 'A personality trait test will also be of help.'

questions.question: the Week 6 lesson cites 1 Corinthians 9:22 and Judges 16:20 as examples of understanding what?
questions.explanation: These verses are cited immediately after 'What you understand about your emotions, desires, ability, etc. is also important'.

questions.question: When you know yourself, you know your strong areas and it is easier to work on your weaknesses and turn them into strengths.
questions.explanation: This is a direct quote from the lesson.

questions.question: To go far in life, you need a good ________ of who you are.
questions.explanation: The conclusion says 'To go far in life, you need a good understanding of who you are.'

questions.question: In the Bible Passage (1 Samuel 17:32-37), who was the young servant confident that he could fight the Philistine?
questions.explanation: The text reads 'Then David said to Saul... your servant will go and fight with this Philistine.'

questions.question: In the Bible Passage (1 Samuel 17:33), who doubted David’s ability to fight because he was just a youth while the opponent was a man of war?
questions.explanation: The passage says 'And Saul said to David, “You are not able to go against this Philistine...' (1 Sam 17:33).

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'Your servant has killed both lion and bear; and this uncircumcised Philistine will be like one of them...'
questions.verse_text: Your servant has killed both lion and bear; and this uncircumcised Philistine will be like one of them...
questions.explanation: This is verse 36 of the main Bible Passage.

questions.question: According to the Bible Passage (1 Samuel 17:37), why was David so confident he would defeat the Philistine?
options: A. Because he had a better sword
B. Because the Lord who delivered him from the lion and bear would deliver him from the Philistine
C. Because he had been training with the army
D. Because the Philistine was old and weak
questions.explanation: David said 'The Lord, who delivered me from the paw of the lion and from the paw of the bear, He will deliver me...' (1 Sam 17:37).

questions.question: And Saul said to David, 'Go, and the ________ be with you!'
questions.explanation: 1 Samuel 17:37 concludes with Saul saying 'Go, and the Lord be with you!'

questions.question: According to Genesis 1:26-27 referenced in Outline I, God has every tiny detail about you.
questions.explanation: The text states 'He has every tiny detail about you (Genesis 1:26-27).'

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'Then David said to Saul, “Let no man’s heart fail because of him; your servant will go and fight with this Philistine.”'
questions.verse_text: Then David said to Saul, “Let no man’s heart fail because of him; your servant will go and fight with this Philistine.”
questions.explanation: This is verse 32, the very first verse of the main Bible Passage.

questions.question: Which verses are cited alongside Luke 10:19 to back up having the understanding of your goals, aspirations, and authority in Christ?
questions.explanation: The text cites '(Luke 10:19; Philippians 3:7-14)' for this point.
